rulings.Case Title: THOMAS NUSSBAUM VS. CAL INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS NUSSBAUM, Motion For Reconsideration. Is denied, no new or different facts, circumstances, or law shown. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD T ROSS VS. WALTER L. ROSS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Walter Ross Should Not Be Excluded From 60 Gates Street
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 17, Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause why Defendant Walter Ross Should Not be Excluded from 60 Gates Street. Is denied. Commissioner Gargano's order does not permit plaintiff to exclude defendant indefinitely from the premises, and does not give plaintiff full possession of the premises. Plaintiff may seek a court order setting forth specific dates and times when plaintiff needs to be present and/or bring third parties onto the premises for repairs, preparations, showing, etc. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID MELANSON VS. S.J. AMOROSO, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Pltf'S Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT LVI EVNIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC. Notice of Motion to Quash Service of Plaintiff's Summons and Complaint. Is denied. Court has jurisdiction over defendant. See Jonson vs. Weinstein (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 954. This ruling does not prejudice defendant's right to raise the issue regarding whether plaintiff has complied with CCP section 474 by way of another motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY J. HOLDMAN et al VS. ALICIA STIDHAM MARSHALL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT 35--45 HOLLIS STREET ASSOCIATES, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to 1st, 3rd, and 4th causes of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action for intentional interference. Plaintiff must plead that defendants acted with the intent to interfere with plaintiff's contract with the buyers by taking the money out of the escrow account. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRAIN RESEARCH LABS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. TOM CLARKE et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT JOSEPH ROTENBERG, Motion of Defendant Joseph Rotenbberg to Recover Attorney's Fees and Costs in the Amount of $12,048.08 as Prevailing Defendant on "Anti-Slapp" Motion to Strike Pursuant to CCP section 425.16(C). Motion granted in its entirety. Hourly billing rate reasonable, and work performed reasonable and necessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HUGH ANTHONY KING VS. NAZAR PROPERTIES III,LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT HUGH KING DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 7th causes of action. Cross-complaint must allege whether the contract was oral or written and attach a copy to the cross-complaint. Cross-complainant must plead a statutory duty to support the 2nd cause of action. As to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, cross-complainant must plead a valid contract between cross-complainant and a third party, and plead damages. The claim of tresspass is deficient because cross-complainant does not allege that cross-defendant did not have a right or interest in the subject property. The 7th cause of action is based upon the 1st and 2nd causes of action, and cross-complainant must allege a duty. Demurrer overruled as to 6th cause of action (nuisance.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY CURTIN et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 26, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT BAC HOME LOANS SERVICING, LP DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (negligence) because a financial institution owes no duty of care to a borrower when its involvement does not exceed the scope of its conventional role as a mere lender. Nymark vs. Heart Fed. Saving & Loan Assn. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3rd 1089. Remainder causes of action sustained with 10 days leave to amend. 1st cause of action (breach of contract) as to alleged written and/or implied contract, plaintiffs must plead the terms of the contract and specify which terms were violated and how. Plaintiffs must allege an oral agreement that is not barred by the statute of frauds. CC section 1624(3). Third cause of action (B&P 17200), 5th cause of action (fraud), and 6th cause of action (fraud), plaintiff must plead fraud with specificity (how, what, when, where, who made the representations and on what authority.) Seventh cause of action (breach of duty of good faith), implied covenant does not impose an affirmative duty on defendant to refrain from enforcing rights expressly given under the contract. No fidiciary duty owed. Eighth cause of action (CC Sec. 2923.5), unclear whether private right of action exists. However, code section does not require modification of loan. Plaintiffs do not allege whether they were contacted or not prior to the commencement of the foreclosure proceedings. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAYED BASHIR RAHIMI VS. TAXI COMMISSION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Petition For Writs Of Adminstrative Mandamus And/Or Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, 5/26,10, line 16, PETN. SAYED RAHIMI, Mtn. to Grant Petn. for Writ of Admin. Mandamus &/or Mandate. Motion denied. Mandate relief under CCP 1005 is available where there exists 1) a clear, present & usually (ministerial duty on the part of respondent, &, 2) a clear present, & beneficial right in the petn. to the performance of that duty. The Crt. determines whether the agency's actions were procedurally unfair, whether the agency failed to follow the procedure & give the notices the law requires, or whether it abused its discretion. Given the language of sec.1076, the Taxi Comm. had the power to create rules & regulations to effectuate the purposes of the Article. The "Police Commission" is granted power to revoke & suspend permits & issue regulations, & the definition section 1076 states the "Police Commission" refers to the "Taxi Commissioner." The Comm. is allowed to weigh various facts & circumstances in deciding whether to revoke, & retains discretion in revocation. The monetary sanctions provisions state they are in addition for the sanctions available under Sec. 1090. The Comm. is empowered to issue monetary sanctions, revocations, and suspensions. The presumptions are not de facto given the force of law, the Comm's actions are not unfair, it followed the relevant legal procedures, & did not abuse its discretion. The Crt. exercises its independent judgment under CCP 1094.5 to determine whether the agency proceeded in excess of its jurisdiction, failed to conduct a fair trial, & abused its discretion. No evid. supports the allegation that the Bd. did not truly exercise de novo review in analyzing the appeal, or that it relied on the presumptions. The transcript of the Bd. of Appeals hearing shows that while the Comm's findings were considered, so was an abundance of additional evidence from interested parties who were of the opinion that petn. was no longer deserving of his medallion & A-card. No error occurred. =(302/CWW)
